_README: CLASSICAL-CONFORMANCE CORPUS. Each entry is a documented horary case whose date/time/place/question/verdict is SOURCED (see `source` + verbatim `sourceQuote`). `lillyVerdict` is the practitioner's own stated verdict; `expectedLean` maps it to Kairos's lean vocabulary; `kairosLean` PINS Kairos's CURRENT live lean for this chart (regression protection, like horary-golden.test.ts). `inScope=false` marks a KNOWN GAP (a chart that uses judgment Kairos does not yet model — natural significators, directional/where reasoning, mundane multi-house synthesis), NOT a bug. Optional `querentHouse` TURNS THE CHART for third-party questions (engine default 1) — the runner passes it through to runCompute; turned cases are IN SCOPE since the engine supports turning. DATA INTEGRITY: do not add a case you cannot source with a date, place and the author's stated verdict. Lilly times reproduced from modern recalculations are LMT for London (IANA Europe/London resolves to London Mean Time for these pre-1847 dates).

cases.source: William Lilly, Christian Astrology (1647), Book III, p. 385 ('A Lady, if marry the Gentleman desired?'); chart data per Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2012-09-30 and 2024-05-02.
cases.sourceQuote: the Match effected, and all within twenty days following, to the content of the sorrowful (but as to me unthankful) Lady.
cases.question: A lady asks whether she shall marry the gentleman she desires.
cases.note: Lilly first leaned NO on a void Moon, then re-judged YES via the Moon's translation of light Mercury->Jupiter and a Jupiter-Saturn link; the marriage occurred within 20 days. CORRECTED MACHINERY NOTE (round 3 audit): at ACCURATE positions there is NO in-orb significator aspect at all (Mercury-Jupiter ~28 deg apart; Lilly's link was a non-Ptolemaic semi-sextile), and the Moon is SEPARATING from both Mercury and Jupiter — the translation Lilly judged by never existed (confirmed by Anthony Louis's 2024 recalculation, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2024-05-02: Lilly's 17th-century tables erred). Kairos: no significator aspect (0), the void Moon (-30) dominates, lean unfavorable. The miss is therefore a DATA-ERA artifact, not an engine gap; the separating-as-denial rule correctly does not fire (no in-orb aspect to be separating). Candidate for inScope=false in a future corpus audit.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'relationshipexample.html' ('Where is this Relationship Going?').
cases.sourceQuote: The relationship is not going anywhere, at least not in a positive way ... the querent reported to me that she had the predicted difficulties with the relationship.
cases.question: Where is this relationship going? (Will the relationship prosper?)
cases.note: Warnock read deterioration (boyfriend drifting to another); outcome confirmed. Kairos sees a translation carrier (Jupiter) but a void Moon nets it to a near-zero uncertain -- a denial/translation-weighting gap. RE-BASELINED (Moon-sequence prohibition implemented): the Moon squares translator Jupiter (~0.6d) before Jupiter's delivery trine to a STATIONING Venus (relative speed ~0, delivery effectively never) — the translation FAILS, leaving the void Moon (-30) dominant: uncertain -> unfavorable. NOW AGREES with Warnock's no. SEPARATING-AS-DENIAL (Lilly CA p. 110): the separating significator sextile with no surviving perfection path now adds -12 (score -25 -> -37); lean unchanged, agreement kept (deeper).

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'jobexample.html' ('Will I Get a Job in 90 Days?').
cases.sourceQuote: it is highly doubtful that I will get a job in the time specified ... Not surprisingly I didn't get a job.
cases.question: Will I get a job within 90 days?
cases.note: Warnock used the 6th house (employment/service) and a weak late-Capricorn Moon to judge NO; no job followed. RE-BASELINED (heavier-collector gate, Lilly CA pp. 110-111): the old Venus 'collection' (+15) fired because Venus (|speed| ~1.18 deg/day) applied to both Saturn (|speed| ~0.05) and Moon (|speed| ~11.9) — but Venus is FASTER than Saturn, violating Lilly's more-ponderous condition; a swift body cannot gather the light of a slower significator. With Venus removed as a collector the score drops -14 → -29 (uncertain → unfavorable). NOW AGREES with Warnock's no — the dominant void-Moon (-30), doubly-debilitated significators (-10), and Saturn retrograde (-4) correctly overwhelm the mutual-reception credit (+15).

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'hegotjobexample.html' ('He Got the Job!').
cases.sourceQuote: The presence of Saturn, 10th ruler dignified, in the 1st, indicates that the querent will get the job ... The querent quickly obtained a new job.
cases.question: Will I get the job?
cases.note: Warnock judged YES from the dignified 10th ruler (Saturn) placed in the 1st. RE-BASELINED (chartJd-correct void detection + perfection by location): the old 'void Moon' was a FALSE POSITIVE of the one-day applying heuristic — the Moon (9.1 Capricorn) perfects an applying square to Venus ~5.6h after the chart, well inside its sign — so the -30 debit drops; and Saturn (dignity +3, direct, clear) in the querent's 1st now earns the perfection-by-location testimony (+15, Lilly CA p. 444, Bonatti p. 33). Score -7 -> +38, uncertain -> favorable. NOW AGREES with Warnock's yes (his own stated testimony, the 10th ruler dignified in the 1st, is exactly the line that fires).

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'gradschooladmissionexample.html' ('Will I get into my chosen graduate school?').
cases.sourceQuote: decidedly negative ... The querent was not admitted to graduate school.
cases.question: Will I be admitted to my chosen graduate school?
cases.note: Weak querent significator separating from the 9th; not admitted. RE-BASELINED (chartJd-correct void detection): the old agreement rested on a FALSE void — the Moon (6.6 Aries) perfects an applying sextile to Saturn ~3h after the chart, inside Aries — so the -30 drops and the score rises -25 -> +5 (uncertain). Now a MISS: Warnock's actual negative testimony (the separating significators as an argument of denial, the weak Sun) was machinery Kairos did not weight; the prior 'agreement' was the right verdict for a wrong reason. SEPARATING-AS-DENIAL implemented (Lilly CA p. 110; Warnock's own stated testimony — 'separating rather than applying aspect'): the separating Sun-Mars sextile with no surviving path now debits -12 (score +5 -> -7). Still uncertain — a lean flip needs a score past -15, beyond the sanctioned affliction-class magnitude — but the score now points in Warnock's direction.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'whenwillwesellourhouseexample.html' ('When Will Our House Sell?').
cases.sourceQuote: Saturn ... retrograde and in detriment ... delays and difficulties ... four months after this question, the querent was unable to sell his house.
cases.question: When will our house sell? (Will the house sell?)
cases.note: Retrograde, detrimented buyer-significator and afflicted price ruler; no sale within four months. Kairos also leans unfavorable (high). Agreement.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'testingexample.html' ('Will I pass my finals and graduate from college?').
cases.sourceQuote: the querent will indeed pass their final exams and graduate ... The querent passed all exams and graduated from college, doing even better than expected!
cases.question: Will I pass my finals and graduate from college?
cases.note: Mercury (querent) applies to a conjunction with dignified Venus (the college/test) in the 9th; passed. Kairos also leans favorable via direct perfection. Agreement. Source page annotates the chart "GMT -3"; the astronomically correct offset for Tel Aviv in June 2003 is UTC+3 (Asia/Jerusalem, used here) — the page notation is a quirk, the chart data matches.

cases.source: Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2011-12-12 ('Pregnant in Athens? A Horary Pregnant with Possibilities').
cases.sourceQuote: the woman was not pregnant; and she ended up needing surgery to remove a cyst that was making her unable to conceive.
cases.question: Am I pregnant?
cases.note: Outcome: not pregnant. RE-BASELINED (chartJd-correct void detection): the old agreement rested on a FALSE void — the Moon (27.97 Pisces) perfects an applying sextile to Mercury ~2h after the chart, inside Pisces — so the -30 drops, and the corrected applying flags surface a Mercury translation Mars -> Moon (+18) with reception (+5): score -25 -> +23, favorable. Now a MISS: Louis's NO came from the 5th-ruler's affliction and the 8th-house surgery picture, which Kairos does not model. NOTE the perfection-by-location gate worked as doctrine requires: Mars (5th ruler) sits in the 1st but in Cancer, its fall (dignity -4), so Bonatti's well-disposed condition denies the +15 that would otherwise pile on. (Real, resolved outcome -- a candidate for the later calibration corpus once enough resolved cases exist.) RE-BASELINED (lighter-planet translation gate, Lilly CA p.111): the Mercury translation that briefly flipped this chart favorable fails the swifter-than-both-significators condition, so it no longer fires; favorable -> uncertain (+5). Moves toward Louis's no but remains a miss. SEPARATING-AS-DENIAL implemented (Lilly CA p. 110): the separating Moon-Mars trine with no surviving path debits -12 (score +5 -> -7); still uncertain — Louis's actual no ran on the 5th-ruler in fall and the 8th-house surgery picture, machinery Kairos does not model.

cases.source: Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2017-10-27 ('An interesting horary chart' -- Will he pass the exam?).
cases.sourceQuote: The Moon ... had just enough strength by accidental fortitudes to transfer the light from Jupiter to Mercury ... The student passed with the minimum passing grade.
cases.question: Will he pass the exam?
cases.note: A narrow YES (minimum passing grade) carried by the Moon translating light Jupiter->Mercury despite the Moon being in detriment. Kairos weights the debilitated Moon negatively and leans unfavorable -- a translation-by-a-weak-Moon gap. Practitioner verdict qualified-but-affirmative -> expectedLean favorable.

cases.source: William Lilly, Christian Astrology (1647), Book III, pp. 212-214 ('Whether the Querent should follow the King or stay with Parliament'); chart data per Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'horarylilly1644.html' (13 April 1644, 8:55 am, London).
cases.sourceQuote: his Majesties designes were betrayed and his cause sinking; and his own losses would be infinite great, even to his utter impoverishment, if he declined not his present resolution.
cases.question: Should the querent leave Parliament's London to follow King Charles at Oxford?
cases.gapReason: Mundane decision judged on NATURAL SIGNIFICATORS and multi-house synthesis (Sun for the King, the King's prospects across the 10th/11th, the querent's estate), not a single quesited-house perfection. The 9th-house mapping here is a rough proxy; Kairos's single-significator perfection model cannot reproduce Lilly's reasoning.
cases.note: Lilly advised against following the King ('do not go to Oxford'); the querent ignored it and lost an estate, confirming Lilly. Logged as a known gap, not a bug. RE-BASELINED (lighter-planet translation gate): the spurious translation died under Lilly's swifter-than-both condition; favorable -> uncertain (+2). SEPARATING-AS-DENIAL (Lilly CA p. 110): the separating square with no surviving path debits -12 (score +2 -> -10), still uncertain. Out-of-scope either way (known gap).

cases.source: William Lilly, England's Propheticall Merline (1644) ('if she should marry the elderly man'); chart data per Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2017-11-30 ('"Should I ...?" questions in horary astrology'): 24 June 1644 (NS), 10:30 AM; London (Lilly's practice; Louis's recalculated chart matches London — Virgo ascendant, Mars ruling and lying in the 8th, both reproduced by this chart).
cases.sourceQuote: after this they broke off all negotiations of marriage, exactly as I told her.
cases.question: A woman asks Lilly if she should marry the elderly man courting her.
cases.note: Lilly found the man's estate encumbered (Mars ruling and placed in the 8th, the man's substance) and told her the match would founder; negotiations broke off exactly as judged. RE-BASELINED (chartJd-correct void detection): the old agreement rested on a FALSE void — the Moon (25.4 Aquarius) perfects an applying sextile to Mars ~6h after the chart, inside Aquarius — so the -30 drops and the score rises -30 -> 0 (uncertain). Now a MISS: Lilly's actual denial (the suitor's encumbered 8th-house substance) is turned-house affliction reasoning Kairos does not yet model. SEPARATING-AS-DENIAL implemented (Lilly CA p. 110): the separating Mercury-Jupiter sextile with no surviving path debits -12 (score 0 -> -12); still uncertain (the band needs < -15, and even -15 would land exactly ON the edge — flipping would require an unsanctioned magnitude, so it is not tuned).

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, The Horary Case Book (2012), 'Will He Marry Me?'; chart data and judgment as reproduced by Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2021-01-30 ('Some thoughts about "prohibition" in horary astrology'): Feb 3, 2004 at 10 PM EST, Washington, DC.
cases.sourceQuote: no, he will not marry the querent
cases.question: Will the man the querent is interested in marry her?
cases.note: A CLASSICAL PROHIBITION denial: the Moon (in Cancer) must first sextile Venus and then oppose Mercury — and leave Cancer for Leo — before it can perfect its square to Mars (the 7th ruler); Venus and Mercury stand in the way, so the marriage is denied. Kairos instead reads the Moon's intervening contacts as an indirect translation path and leans FAVORABLE — the denial spine does not yet model Moon-sequence prohibition (aspects the Moon itself completes before reaching the quesited ruler). The first surviving-prohibition case in the corpus, and it exposes exactly the blind spot it was added to measure. RE-BASELINED (Moon-sequence prohibition implemented): the engine now detects the Moon perfecting an OPPOSITION with translator Mercury (~0.62d) before Mercury delivers its square to Mars (~6.1d), so the translation FAILS and the +18 is suppressed — score +28 -> +10, lean favorable -> uncertain. Still a miss vs Warnock's no (the chart's exalted-Venus reception positives keep it in the dead band), but the anti-correlated favorable is gone.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'pregnancyexample.html' ('Will fertility treatments be successful?'): 'I received and understood the question on 4:04 pm, EDT, March 13, 2002 in Washington, D.C. 38 N 55 77 W 03'.
cases.sourceQuote: Despite a few positive signs, the chart is fairly negative ... While a positive outcome is not impossible, the chart is rather negative.
cases.question: Will fertility treatments be successful?
cases.note: Warnock's qualified-but-negative verdict (weak 8th-house-heavy chart) -> expectedLean unfavorable. Kairos also leans unfavorable (void Moon, no perfection). Agreement. The page's 'EDT' label is a quirk — US daylight time began 2002-04-07, so 4:04 pm on March 13 is EST; verified by recomputation: only the EST chart reproduces the page's stated placements (Sun and Venus in the 8th, Moon on the 8th cusp, Saturn in the 10th).

cases.source: Ivy M. Goldstein-Jacobson, Simplified Horary Astrology (1960), p. 212; chart data and account as reproduced by Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2021-01-30 ('An example of CAZIMI in horary'): 14 Sep 1946 at 17:36 PST, Los Angeles, CA.
cases.sourceQuote: [Ivy] told the querent that there would indeed be a divorce, but the querent told Ivy that she would not give him a divorce.
cases.question: Will there be a divorce?
cases.note: Goldstein-Jacobson judged the divorce WOULD happen despite the querent's refusal; a year later the husband left and obtained an uncontested divorce (Sept 1947). She leaned on Mercury (7th ruler) cazimi-but-afflicted and its tight square to Uranus. Kairos finds no perfection either way and lands at a near-zero uncertain — the 'matter perfects via the 7th-ruler's afflictions' style of judgment nets out flat.

cases.source: Andrew J. Bevan, astronor.com 'horary-physio.htm' ('Will the querent pass her physiotherapy examination?'): 'The question was asked at 17.30 CEST or 15.30 GMT, on Thursday 8. June 1995 in Oslo, 59N55, 10E43'; also reproduced by Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2020-04-15 ('Horary: Will I pass the exam?').
cases.sourceQuote: I pronounced not to be in doubt but that the examination would be passed with ease ... she was examined on the subjects of hip and hand and passed the examination with perfection.
cases.question: Will I pass my physiotherapy exam?
cases.note: Bevan judged YES from Mars (querent) in the 10th before the 'censor' plus the Moon's applying trine to the Sun (10th ruler), and she passed 'with perfection'. RE-BASELINED (chartJd-correct void detection + perfection by location): the old 'void Moon' was a FALSE POSITIVE of the one-day applying heuristic — the Moon (14.95 Libra, NOT late in its sign) perfects an applying trine to the Sun ~4.5h after the chart, inside Libra (exactly Bevan's 'Moon applies a trine to the Sun, Lord of the 10th') — so the -30 drops AND the Moon's +20 trine to the quesited is restored; Mars (dignity +1, direct, clear) in the quesited's 10th adds the weaker location direction (+10, Bonatti: 'the querent is going toward the matter'). Score -35 -> +25, unfavorable -> favorable. NOW AGREES with Bevan's yes, on his own two stated testimonies.

cases.source: Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2020-04-15 ('Horary: Will I pass the exam?'), Karachi case: 'The question was asked in Karachi, Pakistan, on 17 May 2009 at 12:15 pm.' (querent asked about his BROTHER; Louis turns the chart: brother = radical 3rd, brother's 10th = radical 12th).
cases.sourceQuote: This is an argument that the brother will fail the exam ... The outcome was that the brother did not pass the exam, which was a requirement for the job he was seeking, and therefore gave up on pursuing that job.
cases.question: Will my brother pass the exam?
cases.note: FIRST TURNED-CHART CASE in the corpus: the matter is the brother's (radical 3rd), so querentHouse=3 and the exam outcome is the brother's 10th = radical 12th (quesitedHouse=12), matching Louis's own derivation ('the brother's 9th and 10th houses, which are the radical 11th and 12th'). Louis judged failure (Moon ruler of the turned 10th squaring a detrimented-sign Sun; Mercury ruler of the turned 9th peregrine and combust); the brother did not pass. RE-BASELINED (chartJd-correct void detection): the old agreement rested on a FALSE void — the Moon (25.9 Aquarius) perfects an applying square to the Sun ~10 MINUTES after the chart, inside Aquarius — so the -30 drops and the score rises -17 -> +13 (uncertain). Now a MISS: Louis read that very Moon-square-Sun-in-detriment as his argument of FAILURE (a hard aspect to a debilitated ruler as denial), a negative reading Kairos does not yet model.
